headline: Only 3 U.S. Airlines Can Remain Profitable at Current Oil Prices
summary: Surging oil prices linked to the Iran conflict are expected to squeeze U.S. airline profits, especially since many carriers reduced fuel hedging and are now highly exposed to jet fuel price spikes.

headline: American Airlines Drops 4% as the Airline Sector Hits an Air Pocket
summary: American Airlines Group (NASDAQ:AAL) shares slipped 4% in Thursday trading, falling below $11 as a perfect storm of macro fears, surging oil prices, and company-specific news lands all at once. The broader airline sector is getting hit hard today, but American is absorbing the most punishment. The S&P 500 also headed lower as stagflation fears ... headline: Airlines might not even flinch at rising fuel costs, airfare: Expert
summary: US airline operators — including American (AAL), United (UAL), Delta (DAL), Southwest (LUV), and JetBlue Airways (JBLU) — are coming under pressure this week as Middle East tensions continue to pressure oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F), threatening to send jet fuel costs soaring. This comes after a partial government shutdown has led to massive slowdowns at major airports' TSA checkpoints (Transportation Security Administration). Steve Trent, an independent aviation specialist, comes on Market Catalysts to discuss what this all means for travel demand trends and airfare prices. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Airline stocks slide as oil surges past $100 on Iran tanker attacks
summary: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) led declines, dropping 2.7% to $40.73 after ending its fuel hedging program in 2025, leaving it fully exposed to spot market pricing. Delta Air Lines Inc (NYSE:DAL) fell 2% to $57.97, while United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) declined 2.2% to $88.73. American Airlines Group (BMV:AAL) fell 2% to $10.82. European airline stocks suffered steeper losses, falling between 2.4% and 5.8% Thursday morning, with Lufthansa, Air France-KLM, easyJet, IAG, Wizz Air, Norwegian Air, and R
